perturbation.original_text: Borrower shall maintain the Information in confidence and shall not disclose the information to any third party with out the consent of Lending Agent; provided, however, that Borrower may disclose the Information to the extent required by Sample 2 subpoena, court order, request from a regulatory body with jurisdiction over Borrower, or as otherwise required by law or regulation.
perturbation.changed_text: Borrower shall maintain the Information in confidence and shall not disclose the information to any third party without the consent of Lending Agent; provided, however, that Borrower may disclose the Information to the extent required by subpoena, court order, request from a regulatory body with jurisdiction over Borrower, or as otherwise required by any internal policy.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'law or regulation' with 'any internal policy' allows disclosure based on subjective internal standards, conflicting with legal requirements for confidentiality exceptions.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 24b-2 outlines procedures for confidential treatment of information filed with the SEC. Disclosing information based solely on 'any internal policy' undermines these legal protections and disclosure obligations.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.24b-2 - Nondisclosure of information filed with the Commission and with any exchange. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.24b-2 - Nondisclosure of information filed with the Commission and with any exchange. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.24b-2 Nondisclosure of information filed with the Commission and with any exchange. Except as otherwise provided in this rule, confidential treatment requests shall be submitted in paper format only, whether or not the filer is required to submit a filing in electronic format . (a) Any person filing any registration statement , report, application, statement, correspondence, notice or other document (herein referred to as the material filed ) pursuant to the Act may make written objection to the public disclosure of any information contained therein in accordance with the procedure set forth below. The procedure provided in this rule shall be the exclusive means of requesting confidential treatment of information required to be filed under the Act. (b) Except as otherwise provided in paragraphs (g), (h), (i), (j), and (k) of this section , the person shall omit from material filed the portion thereof which it desires to keep undisclosed (hereinafter called the confidential portion). In lieu thereof, it shall indicate at the appropriate place in the material filed that the confidential portion has been so omitted and filed separately with the Commission. The person shall file with the copies of the material filed with the Commission: (1) One copy of the confidential portion, marked “Confidential Treatment,” of the material filed with the Commission. The copy shall contain an appropriate identification of the item or other requirement involved and, notwithstanding that the confidential portion does not constitute the whole of the answer, the entire answer thereto; except that in the case wher
perturbation.justification: The change from 'law or regulation' to 'any internal policy' directly contradicts the intent of Rule 24b-2, which governs the nondisclosure of information filed with the SEC. The original text allowed disclosure only when legally mandated, while the changed text permits disclosure based on an internal policy. This undermines the legal obligation for confidential treatment as outlined in 17 CFR § 240.24b-2, which provides a specific procedure for requesting confidential treatment of information. Allowing internal policies to dictate disclosure circumvents the legal framework designed to protect sensitive information filed with the Commission.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, with complete disregard to its conflict of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: While parties can generally choose governing law, specifying 'complete disregard' for conflict of law principles might lead a court to question the validity of the choice, particularly if it appears designed to circumvent consumer protection laws or other mandatory rules that New York would apply. Some states' laws have a better reputation for benefiting corporations over private individuals or consumers.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 187 of the Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws allows parties to choose the governing law, but this choice is not absolute. It is subject to limitations where the chosen law violates a fundamental policy of a state with a materially greater interest in the issue.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes the governing law from New York to Delaware and includes the phrase 'complete disregard to its conflict of law principles.' The Law Explanation states that Section 187 of the Restatement (Second) of Conflict of Laws allows parties to choose the governing law, but this choice is not absolute and is subject to limitations. The Perturbation Explanation highlights the concern that 'complete disregard' might lead a court to question the validity of the choice. While no specific law snippets are available to support a direct violation, the potential conflict with established principles of conflict of laws and the intent to circumvent consumer protection, combined with the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation definition, creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings, oral or written, relating to th e subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings, oral or written, relating to the subject matter hereof. However, any implied duties of good faith and fair dealing are explicitly disclaimed.
perturbation.explanation: Attempting to disclaim the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing is problematic. While the extent of the duty varies by jurisdiction (especially under New York law), many courts will not enforce a complete waiver, as it would essentially allow a party to act arbitrarily and undermine the contract's purpose.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith in the performance and enforcement of every contract or duty within the UCC. While this contract may not fall entirely under the UCC, the principle is widely recognized, and disclaiming it entirely undermines the integrity of contractual relationships, possibly violating state contract law principles related to unconscionability or fundamental fairness.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text attempts to disclaim the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing. The Law Explanation states that UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explicitly states, "Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." Disclaiming this duty, even if the contract doesn't fall entirely under the UCC, undermines the integrity of contractual relationships, creating a legal contradiction as the modified text directly contradicts a legally recognized obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Borrower acknowledges that, except with respect to information regarding loans entered into on behalf of specifi c Principals, Lending Agent did not create or compile the Information and is merely forwarding information obtained from Principals. Accordingly, Borrower acknowledges that Lending Agent makes no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy or comp leteness of the Information and that Borrower is not relying on Lending Agent to have performed any review of the Information for accuracy or completeness.
perturbation.changed_text: Borrower acknowledges that, except with respect to information regarding loans entered into on behalf of specific Principals, Lending Agent did not create or compile the Information and is merely forwarding information obtained from Principals. Accordingly, Borrower acknowledges that Lending Agent makes no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that Borrower is not relying on Lending Agent to have performed any review of the Information for accuracy or completeness. However, Lending agent confirms that the data is not misleading.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, Lending agent confirms that the data is not misleading' creates a direct contradiction. Previously, the agent disclaimed all warranties about accuracy or completeness. Now, they are implicitly warranting that information is not misleading. A misleading statement does not always mean inaccurate, for example, it could be an omission. This would make the clause legally ambiguous.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 349 prohibits deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any business, trade or commerce or in the furnishing of any service in this state. The agent's statement about the data not being misleading, while disclaiming accuracy, could be construed as a deceptive practice if the information is in fact misleading, even if technically not inaccurate. The misleading information must cause damage for it to be a violation.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a contradiction because while the original text disclaims warranties about accuracy and completeness, the changed text adds a confirmation that the data is 'not misleading.' This contradicts the disclaimer because a statement can be misleading even if it's technically accurate. The Law Explanation mentions Section 349 prohibiting deceptive acts or practices. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to Article 22-A Consumer Protection From Deceptive Acts and Practices. This snippet directly relates to consumer protection from deceptive practices, aligning with the agent's altered statement potentially misleading the borrower and creating a legal contradiction.
